study: SG-MO-028 causative — hand-adjudication of the seeded class-10 (-ay) sample
sample_kind: freq-weighted draw of distinct class-10 lemma types (estimates token-mass composition)

categories.caus: transparent verbal causative — -aya- stem derived from an attested simpler verbal root, with 'cause to X' / factitive semantics (kāray←kṛ, darśay←dṛś, janay←jan)
categories.curadi: primary curādi (class-X) or denominal-class-10 verb — the -aya- form is the base lexical verb, with no simpler verbal root (kathay, cintay, pūjay, bhakṣay); a FALSE POSITIVE if class-10 is read as 'causative'

tally.note: false-positive rate of reading class-10 = causative; freq-weighted sample → estimates the primary-curādi share of the token mass (~18 %). Corroborated by the mechanical de-strengthening lower bound (79.7 % of bucket mass has an attested base root → ~20 % residual).

notable_false_positives.why: primary curādi 'narrate' (or denominal from kathā); no simpler verbal root — the single largest class-10 lemma
primary curādi 'worship'; class-10 base verb, not a causative
